backlog.title: scaffold skill/kypr/ directory structure
backlog.description: Create the skill/kypr/ directory tree with placeholder files. Structure: skill/kypr/plugin.json, projections.json, install.py, scripts/verify_encryption.py, scripts/kypr.py, adapters/claude-code/ (empty), workspace-state/ (empty). No logic yet — scaffold only.
acceptance_criteria: skill/kypr/ exists with all required subdirectories and placeholder files.
am_i_done.py passes on a feature branch.

backlog.title: write plugin.json and projections.json
backlog.description: Write skill/kypr/plugin.json (name, version, description metadata) and skill/kypr/projections.json (flat JSON array of {source, target} pairs covering all files install.py must copy into a target repo).
acceptance_criteria: skill/kypr/plugin.json exists with name, version, and description fields.
skill/kypr/projections.json exists as a valid JSON array of {source, target} objects.
Every file that install.py should copy is listed in projections.json.

backlog.title: write verify_encryption.py
backlog.description: Write skill/kypr/scripts/verify_encryption.py. Must: (1) detect whether git-crypt is installed, (2) detect whether the repo has git-crypt configured, (3) exit 0 if correctly configured, exit 1 with a clear error message if not. This check is not optional and cannot be made conditional. It is called at session start and by install.py before projecting any files.
acceptance_criteria: verify_encryption.py exits 0 in a repo with git-crypt correctly configured.
verify_encryption.py exits 1 with a human-readable error in a repo without git-crypt.
No flag or environment variable can suppress the check.

backlog.title: write install.py
backlog.description: Write skill/kypr/install.py. Must: (1) call verify_encryption.py and exit non-zero if it fails, (2) read projections.json, (3) copy each source file to its target path in the calling repo, (4) enforce path containment (no source or target escaping skill/kypr/ or the target repo root respectively), (5) skip .work/ if it already exists in the target repo, (6) be idempotent. No symlinks. No tier selection.
acceptance_criteria: install.py copies all files listed in projections.json to their target paths.
install.py exits non-zero if verify_encryption.py fails.
install.py is idempotent — running it twice produces the same result.
install.py skips .work/ if already present in target repo.
Path containment checks reject any source/target outside expected roots.
am_i_done.py passes on a feature branch.

backlog.title: write kypr.py core entry point
backlog.description: Write skill/kypr/scripts/kypr.py. Must: (1) accept --status to print the active workspace name, (2) accept --workspace <name> to switch the active workspace, (3) read/write workspace state from workspace-state/ (encrypted via git-crypt), (4) call verify_encryption.py at startup and refuse to proceed if it fails. No harness-specific code — all I/O is plain stdout/stderr. kypr.py does NOT discover or load adapters; adapters are harness-level configuration (settings.json, startup.sh). Adding a second harness adapter requires no changes to kypr.py or any file outside adapters/.
acceptance_criteria: kypr.py --status prints the active workspace name.
kypr.py --workspace <name> switches the active workspace.
kypr.py calls verify_encryption.py and exits non-zero if it fails.
No harness-specific code in kypr.py — it is agent-agnostic.

backlog.title: write Claude Code adapter (adapters/claude-code/)
backlog.description: Write the Claude Code harness adapter in skill/kypr/adapters/claude-code/. Must contain: settings.json (hook definitions wiring verify_encryption.py to SessionStart), startup.sh (session startup: calls verify_encryption.py, prints active workspace). No kypr core logic here — only harness wiring. Thin by design.
acceptance_criteria: adapters/claude-code/settings.json defines a SessionStart hook that calls verify_encryption.py.
adapters/claude-code/startup.sh calls verify_encryption.py and exits non-zero if it fails.
No Kypr core logic in the adapter — only wiring.
A second harness adapter could be added without modifying any file outside adapters/.

backlog.title: define workspace-state/ structure
backlog.description: Define the workspace-state/ directory structure inside skill/kypr/. Must: (1) support at minimum personal and professional workspaces, (2) store the active workspace name as plaintext metadata, (3) be designed so all personal-data files (conversation context, session history, references, workspace content — per requirements.md §5) are in paths covered by the .gitattributes git-crypt filter. Document the structure with a README. No data written yet — structure and schema only. Depends on WORK-0013 (.gitattributes) being complete.
acceptance_criteria: workspace-state/ layout documented (README or inline comments).
Structure supports at minimum two isolated workspaces (personal, professional).
All personal-data files (context, history, references, content) are in paths covered by .gitattributes git-crypt filter.
Active workspace name (metadata) is stored plaintext.
.gitattributes git-crypt rules for workspace-state/ paths exist (WORK-0013).

backlog.title: create .gitattributes with git-crypt filter rules
backlog.description: Create .gitattributes (or extend if present) with filter=git-crypt diff=git-crypt rules covering all personal-data paths in workspace-state/. Must be in place before any personal data files are committed. Rules must cover the paths defined in WORK-0012's structure.
acceptance_criteria: .gitattributes exists with at least one filter=git-crypt rule.
All personal-data paths from WORK-0012 are covered by a filter=git-crypt rule.
verify_encryption.py check (2) — '.gitattributes contains at least one filter=git-crypt rule' — passes after this item.

backlog.title: fix ReDoS in check_pr_template.py
backlog.description: Pre-existing ReDoS in check_pr_template.py: _FENCED_BLOCK regex (line 58) and section_content pattern (line 77) use unbounded .* with re.DOTALL — catastrophic backtracking possible on adversarial input. Fix by bounding the match (possessive quantifier or atomic group equivalent) or by using a line-by-line parser instead of a single regex over the full body.
acceptance_criteria: strip_noise() and section_content() do not exhibit catastrophic backtracking on inputs with many nested backtick or heading sequences.
am_i_done.py passes.

completed.title: define phase 2 build backlog
completed.description: Draft work items in .work/work.json for the full skill/kypr/ build (phase 2). Items must cover: skill directory scaffold, verify_encryption.py, install.py (with projections.json manifest), kypr.py, adapters/claude-code/ wiring, workspace-state/ structure. Each item must reference docs/requirements.md or ADR-001 as its source.
acceptance_criteria: Phase 2 work items exist in .work/work.json backlog.
Each item references requirements.md or an ADR.
Items collectively cover all phase-2 DoD criteria.

completed.title: update CLAUDE.md with requirements context
completed.description: CLAUDE.md was created as phase infrastructure. Once WORK-0001 (requirements.md) is complete, update CLAUDE.md to replace the 'NOT YET CREATED' placeholders with accurate references to docs/requirements.md content.
acceptance_criteria: CLAUDE.md references docs/requirements.md and reflects accurate project description.
am_i_done.py passes on a feature branch.

completed.title: ADR-001: distribution model decision
completed.description: Record the architecture decision on whether Kypr follows hedl's distribution model exactly or adapts it. Must reference docs/hedl-study.md as evidence. Write docs/adr/ADR-001-distribution-model.md.
acceptance_criteria: docs/adr/ADR-001-distribution-model.md exists.
ADR references hedl-study.md findings.
Decision is explicit: follow exactly, or adapt with documented reasons.

completed.title: hedl distribution model study
completed.description: Study neilgfoster/hedl to understand its skill delivery pattern for Kypr to follow. Hedl is a reference example, not a framework. Read: SKILL.md, plugin.json, tiers.json, adapters.json, install.py, the skill directory structure, and how files land in target repos. Document what conventions Kypr should adopt, what it may adapt, and any known constraints (e.g. symlinks vs copies for CI compatibility). Output: docs/hedl-study.md.
acceptance_criteria: docs/hedl-study.md exists.
Document covers: directory structure, install mechanism, projection model (how files land in target repos), known constraints, and open questions for Kypr.

completed.title: write requirements document
completed.description: Produce docs/requirements.md from the operator-provided requirements. Cover: what Kypr is (personal assistant framework, agent skill, persistent/context-aware, isolated workspaces); security model (git-crypt mandatory, verify at session start, refuse if not configured, no bypass); distribution (hedl delivery model); agent compatibility (agent-agnostic core, thin harness wiring); workspace isolation (personal and professional); session model (interactive only, no background processes, no servers).
acceptance_criteria: docs/requirements.md exists and covers all six requirement areas above.
Each section is traceable to the operator-provided requirements text.

completed.title: build PR template check for this repo
completed.description: Hedl ships check_pr_template.py as a static placeholder that validates Hedl's own section schema. This item replaces it with a repo-specific version derived from the actual .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md. Implementation: read .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md, extract every '## Heading' section, generate .github/scripts/check_pr_template.py that validates a PR body has each section filled with non-placeholder content. The generated script uses the same is_placeholder / section_content pattern as the static version. Commit the result. The gate's template check skips until this file exists.
acceptance_criteria: .github/scripts/check_pr_template.py exists and validates the sections present in .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md.
am_i_done.py --pr N template check runs (no longer skips) after this item.
